project.title: Relative quantification of 15N labeled acinus-2 pinin-1 and 14N labeled Col through Data Dependent Acquisition to obtain peptide information for targeted Parallel Reaction Monitoring experiment
project.projectDescription: To examine the properties of 14N and 15N labeled peptides using Parallel Reaction Monitoring (PRM) on a high resolution, high accuracy mass spectrometer we first set up this DDA experiment. The purpose of this experiment was to choose peptides that are good candidates for targeted quantification. The retention times, mass, and charge from 42 candidate peptides were acquired for targeted quantification.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The WT and acinus-2 pinin-1 plants were grown on Hoagland medium containing 14N or 15N (1.34 g/L Hogland’s No. 2 salt mixture without nitrogen, 6 g/L Phytoblend, and 1 g/L KNO3 or 1 g/L K15NO3 (Cambridge Isotope Laboratories), pH 5.8). Proteins were extracted from two samples (one 14N-labeled Col, one 15N-labeled acinus-2 pinin-1) followed with protocols as described (Xu et al. 2017) with slight modification. Briefly, samples were first extracted individually using SDS sample buffer (0.1 M Tris⋅HCl, pH 8.0; 2% (wt/vol) SDS; 20 mM EGTA; 20 mM EDTA; 1.2% (vol/vol) Triton X-100; 2x protease inhibitor), then the protein concentration of each sample was measured by BCA assay kit (Thermo Fisher), subsequently followed mixture have 1:1 protein concentration mixture. Then Proteins were further extracted by cold phenol extraction. Proteins were digested with trypsin and the resulting peptides were de-salted using Sep-Pak waters C18 centrifuge columns. The peptides were analyzed on a Q-Exactive HF mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher) equipped with an Easy LC 1200 UPLC liquid chromatography system (Thermo Fisher).   Peptides were first trapped using trapping column Acclaim PepMap 100 (75 uM x 2cm, nanoViper 2Pk, C18, 3 µm, 100A), then separated using analytical column Acclaim PepMap RSLC (75um x25cm, nanoViper, C18, 2 µm, 100A) (Thermo Fisher). The flow rate was 300 nL/min, and a 120-min gradient was used. Peptides were eluted by a gradient from 3 to 28% solvent B (80% (v/v) acetonitrile/0.1% (v/v) formic acid) over 100 min and from 28 to 44% solvent B over 20 min, followed by a short wash at 90% solvent B. For DDA acquisition, the precursor scan was from mass-to-charge ratio (m/z) 375 to 1600 and top 20 most intense multiply charged precursors were selected for fragmentation. Peptides were fragmented with higher-energy collision dissociation (HCD) with normalized collision energy (NCE) 27.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: A peak list was generated from the raw data (Q20210927_15.raw) using an in-house script PAVA. The peak list was searched using Protein Prospector using TAIR10 (concatenated with random sequences to allow estimation of a false discovery rate) as the reference proteome. The fixed modification set was Carbamidomethyl cysteine. Variable modifications allowed in the search include Oxidation of methionine, N-terminal methionine loss, N-terminal Glutamate to Pyroglutamate Conversion, and N-terminal acetylation. The data was searched with a 10 ppm tolerance for precursor ions and 20 ppm for fragment ions. Peptide and protein FDRs were set as 0.01 and 0.05. 15N labeled peptides were searched separately using the same peak list and reference proteome as before. All parameters of the 15N labeled peptide search were the same except substituting the appropriate 15N labeled fixed and variable mods. 15N labeling efficiency was calculated to be 96.1% by manually comparing theoretical isotopic peaks from 9 of the most abundant peptides and averaging them. Quantification: Quantification was done using Protein Prospector which uses the previous 15N and 14N searches along with the 15N labeling efficiency to calculate the L/H ratio.

labPIs.name: Shouling Xu

references.referenceLine: Reyes AV, Shrestha R, Baker PR, Chalkley RJ, Xu SL. Application of Parallel Reaction Monitoring in <sup>15</sup>N Labeled Samples for Quantification. Front Plant Sci. 2022 13:832585

Characteristics[Specimen]: separated by the SDS-PAGE gel with a very short run (~3 cm)
Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate]: one 14N-labeled Col sample −1, two of 15N-labeled Col samples −2, −3, one 15N-labeled acinus-2 pinin-1 sample −4, and two of 14N-labeled acinus-2 pinin-1 sample −5 and −6
Comment[Instrument]: high resolution, high mass accuracy Orbitrap mass spectrometer
Comment[Separation]: 50 cm column ES803 (50 cm × 75 μm ID, PepMap RSLC C18, 2 μm, Thermo Fisher)
trapping column Acclaim PepMap 100 (75 μM × 2 cm, C18, 3 μm, Thermo Fisher)
analytical column Acclaim PepMap RSLC (75 μm × 25 cm, C18, 2 μm, Thermo Fisher)
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: Peptides were fragmented with higher-energy collision dissociation (HCD).
FactorValue[Treatment]: one forward sample F1 (14N Col/15N acinus-2 pinin-1, Mix 1 + 4) and two reverse samples R1 and R2 (14N acinus-2 pinin-1/15N Col, Mix 2 + 5, and Mix 3 + 6)
